title: GPT-4: Multimodal Frontier
concept: GPT-4 scaled further, introducing multimodal capability (visual input) and advanced logical reasoning to the frontier. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Mixture of Experts (MoE).'

exercises.prompt: What does it mean when we say an AI model is 'multimodal'?

options.text: It can process both text and visual inputs

exercises.explain: Multimodal means the model can handle multiple types of communication inputs, such as text and visual images.

cards.title: AI Gets a Pair of Eyes
cards.body: Before GPT-4, AI was mostly blind—it could only read and write text. GPT-4 changed the game by adding eyes, allowing it to analyze images, diagrams, and photos just like a human.

cards.title: What is Multimodality?
cards.body: GPT-4 is a multimodal AI model. This means it can accept both text and image inputs and generate text outputs, allowing it to read charts, explain memes, and solve visual puzzles.

cards.title: The Eyes of the Mind
cards.body: Imagine GPT-3 was like listening to an audiobook. You could hear the story perfectly, but you couldn't see the pictures. GPT-4 is like upgrading to a movie: it can process both the written script and the visual scenes at the same time.

cards.title: A Chef in Your Camera
cards.body: If you upload a photo of your open fridge, GPT-4 can analyze the ingredients it sees (like eggs, milk, and spinach) and write a step-by-step recipe for a spinach omelette.

cards.title: Seeing vs. Drawing
cards.body: Many people think GPT-4 generates images because it can see them. In reality, the standard GPT-4 model only takes images as input and outputs text (like code or descriptions). It uses partners like DALL-E to generate new images.

cards.title: Text vs. Vision
cards.body: Let's compare how old text-only models and new multimodal models process a photo of a math problem.

left.text: Needs you to type out the math equation using keyboard symbols.

right.text: Lets you take a photo of the math worksheet and solves it directly.

exercises.prompt: What is a major new feature introduced in GPT-4 compared to older GPT models?

options.text: It can accept both images and text as inputs
It is much smaller than GPT-3

exercises.explain: Unlike previous text-only versions, GPT-4 can accept both images and text as inputs.

exercises.prompt: True or False: GPT-4 can read hand-written text directly from a photo you upload.
exercises.explain: GPT-4's visual understanding allows it to read handwritten notes, diagrams, and charts.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing term to describe GPT-4's sensory abilities.
exercises.sentence: Because GPT-4 can understand both images and text, it is called a multimodal model.
exercises.explain: A model that can process more than one type of input (like text and images) is called multimodal.

exercises.prompt: Three students are discussing how to use GPT-4 to write a report based on a line chart image. Who is correct?

options.text: Reviewer A: 'GPT-4 is purely text-based, so you must type out all the numbers from the chart yourself.'
Reviewer B: 'GPT-4 needs a human coder to translate the image into Python code first.'
Reviewer C: 'GPT-4 can look at the chart image directly and explain the trend.'

exercises.explain: Reviewer C is correct because GPT-4 has visual capabilities, allowing it to read chart data directly from an uploaded image.

exercises.prompt: How does GPT-4 natively understand both images and text without relying on external description tools?

options.text: By converting all images into long text descriptions first
By only reading the file names of the images
By native multimodal processing of text and pixel patterns together
By searching the web for matching photos

exercises.explain: GPT-4 brought vision and superior reasoning, but running such a massive model is incredibly expensive. Next up: Mixture of Experts (MoE), which routes tasks to smaller specialist sub-networks!
